{{Distinguish|text=[[Oman Club]], a football team within the Oman Professional League}}

| Regional cup first    = [[2004 AFC Asian Cup|2004]]
| Regional cup best     = Round of 16 ([[2019 AFC Asian Cup|2019]])
| 2ndRegional name      = [[WAFF Championship]]
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 5
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[2008 WAFF Championship|2008]]
| 2ndRegional cup best  = Third place ([[2012 WAFF Championship|2012]])
| 3rdRegional name      = [[Arabian Gulf Cup]]
| 3rdRegional cup apps  = 24
| 3rdRegional cup first = [[1st Arabian Gulf Cup|1974]]

}}
The '''Oman national football team''' ({{langx|ar|منتخب عُمَان لِكُرَةُ الْقَدَم}}) represents [[Oman]] in men's international [[association football|football]], and is controlled by the [[Oman Football Association]]. Oman has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]], however, they have made six appearances in the [[AFC Asian Cup]].

==History==
===World Cup and Asian Cup===
The Oman national football team has made notable efforts in international competitions but has not qualified for the FIFA World Cup. Their best performance in the Asian Cup was reaching the round of 16 in 2019. The team has shown improvement over the years, and their participation in various qualifiers and tournaments reflects their growing competitiveness in regional football.

===Gulf Cup===
The mid-1990s under the OFA chairmanship of Sheikh Saif bin Hashil Al-Maskary saw Oman achieve positive results on the Asian stage. Former Omani captain, [[Hani Al-Dhabit]] was awarded the [[RSSSF]] 2001 World Top Scorer, with 22 goals;<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/iffhs-scor.html |title=– Al-Dhabit scored 22 goals in 2001 |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=17 August 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170817121301/http://www.rsssf.com/miscellaneous/iffhs-scor.html#01w |url-status=live }}</ref> the most goals scored by a player who won the World Top Scorer award and being the third [[Arab]] and the first [[Oman|Omani]] to win the award.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://forum.montadayatbh.net/showthread.php?t=7576 |title=– 3rd Arab to receive the award, and first Omani. |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=19 March 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210319044117/https://www.google.com/dp/ads?r=m&cpp=0&client=dp-sedo80_3ph&channel=exp-0051%2Cauxa-control-1%2C8810114&hl=en&adtest=off&adsafe=low&type=3&swp=as-drid-2383353299994854&uiopt=false&oe=UTF-8&ie=UTF-8&fexp=21404%2C17300494%2C17300496%2C17300598&format=r6&num=0&output=afd_ads&domain_name=forum.montadayatbh.net&v=3&adext=as1%2Csr1&bsl=8&u_his=50&u_tz=0&dt=1616128877203&u_w=1600&u_h=1000&biw=1085&bih=784&psw=1085&psh=1039&frm=0&uio=ff3fa2st22sa14lt40sl1sr1-&cont=rb-default&csize=w293h0&inames=master-1&jsv=37802&rurl=http%3A%2F%2Fforum.montadayatbh.net%2Fshowthread.php%3Ft%3D7576 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

In the [[15th Arabian Gulf Cup|2002 Arabian Gulf Cup]] which was held in [[Saudi Arabia]], Oman finished in 5th place and under the captaincy of [[Dhofar (football club)|Dhofar]]'s [[Hani Al Dhabit|Hani Al-Dhabit]], Oman defeated 9-time winners<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?g=51&winners=true |title=9-time winners of the Gulf Cup |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=20 February 2009 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090220181230/http://kooora.com/default.aspx?g=51&winners=true |url-status=live }}</ref> [[Kuwait national football team|Kuwait]]. The match had ended 3–1 with Al-Dhabit scoring a [[Hat-trick (football)|hat-trick]]. Hani netted a goal against [[Bahrain national football team|Bahrain]] and a consolation goal in a 2–1 loss against [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=160&stage=1&sch=true |title=– match results from the 2002 Gulf Cup. |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=1 August 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180801034509/http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=160&stage=1&sch=true |url-status=live }}</ref> At the end of the tournament, Hani was the only Omani to score goals for his nation and was awarded for being the top goal scorer of the competition, with 5 goals.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=160&scorers=true |title=– Hani Al-Dhabit, top goalscorer of the 2002 Gulf Cup with a total of 5 goals. |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=1 August 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180801034517/http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=160&scorers=true |url-status=live }}</ref>

In the [[17th Arabian Gulf Cup|2004 Arabian Gulf Cup]] which was held in [[Doha]], Oman reached the final for the first time in the team's history, which was eventually lost to the hosts [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]] in a penalty shootout after the goalkeeping sensation Ali Al-Habsi missed a penalty. [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]] won the match 6–5 on penalties after the match had ended 1–1 at normal time. Oman's [[Amad Al-Hosni]] was awarded the "Top Goalscorer" award of the tournament with a total of 4 goals.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.goalzz.com/main.aspx?c=737&scorers=true |title=Gulf Cup 17 – Qatar – goalzz.com |access-date=10 August 2010 |archive-date=7 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211107160243/https://www.goalzz.com/main.aspx?c=737&scorers=true |url-status=live }}</ref>

In the [[18th Arabian Gulf Cup|2007 Arabian Gulf Cup]] which was held in [[United Arab Emirates]], the team reached the final for a second consecutive time and lost 1–0 to the hosts [[United Arab Emirates national football team|United Arab Emirates]]. It had maintained an undefeated record throughout the competition excluding the final.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2432&stage=1&sch=true |title=– Oman's performance in the 2007 Gulf Cup. |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=1 August 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180801064058/http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2432&stage=1&sch=true |url-status=live }}</ref> Ali Al-Habsi had received the "Best Goalkeeper of the Gulf Cup" award<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2432&scorers=true |title=– Al-Habsi receiving the Best Goalkeeper award. |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=7 February 2009 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090207171949/http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2432&scorers=true |url-status=live }}</ref> for the third consecutive time in a row, the most won by any goalkeeper in the 40 years of the Gulf Cup tournament. Oman had tied United Arab Emirates in goal-scoring with 9 goals each after the tournament.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2432&stage=1&sch=true |title=– Oman's goal scoring record in the 18th Gulf Cup. |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=1 August 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180801064058/http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2432&stage=1&sch=true |url-status=live }}</ref>

After two consecutive defeats in the Gulf Cup final, Oman won the [[19th Arabian Gulf Cup|2009 Arabian Gulf Cup]] tournament as hosts by defeating [[Saudi Arabia national football team|Saudi Arabia]] in a penalty shootout. Oman won the match 6–5 on penalties after the match had ended 0–0 at extra time. Oman maintained a clean-sheet throughout the tournament.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2265&stage=1&sch=true |title=– Oman's 19th Gulf Cup record. |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=14 March 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100314213401/http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2265&stage=1&sch=true |url-status=live }}</ref> The tournament in [[Muscat]] was the first for [[Hassan Rabia]], who managed to score 4 goals, earning the "Top Goalscorer" award.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2265&scorers=true |title=Hassan Rabia—19th Gulf Cup top goalscorer. |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-date=17 February 2009 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090217123644/http://kooora.com/default.aspx?c=2265&scorers=true |url-status=live }}</ref> Ali Al-Habsi received his fourth consecutive "Best Goalkeeper Award".<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.bwfc.co.uk/page/ProfilesDetail/0,,1004~36152,00.html |title=– BWFC – Ali 4 time winner of Best Goalkeeper award. |access-date=5 January 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091231042914/http://www.bwfc.co.uk/page/ProfilesDetail/0,,1004~36152,00.html |archive-date=31 December 2009 |url-status=dead  }}</ref>

==Kit==

{{Football kit box |align= right | | pattern_la = _whitelower | pattern_b = _oman199798away | pattern_ra = | leftarm = D41C21 | body = D41C21 | rightarm = D41C21 | shorts = D41C21 | socks = D41C21 | title = The "[[confetti]]ed" kit provided by Thai firm [[Grand Sport Group|Grand Sport]] during Oman's attempted qualification for the [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998 World Cup]]}}

The team signed a contract in 2006 with [[Gulf Air]].<ref>{{cite web | url = http://www.gulfair.com/English/aboutgulfair/Pages/History.aspx#2006 | title = Gulf Air signs deal with Oman | website = gulfair.com | access-date = 24 January 2010 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20130525172923/http://www.gulfair.com/English/aboutgulfair/Pages/History.aspx#2006 | archive-date = 25 May 2013 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url = http://www.englishsabla.com/forum/showthread.php?38562-Gulf-Air-Signs-Sponsorship-Deal-With-Oman-Football-Association | title = Gulf Air Signs Sponsorship Deal With Oman Football Association − SportsOman reports the signed deal | website = englishsabla.com | publisher = Sports Oman | date = 21 May 2006 | access-date = 24 January 2010 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120721204607/http://www.englishsabla.com/forum/showthread.php?38562-Gulf-Air-Signs-Sponsorship-Deal-With-Oman-Football-Association | archive-date = 21 July 2012 }}</ref> 

On 9 May 2012, the Oman Football Association launched the new official team kit to be worn by Oman in their push for [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC fourth round]]. The kit was launched together with a new OFA logo. The new kit was designed for Oman by Taj Oman, an Oman-based company.<ref>{{cite web | url = http://theweek.co.om/disCon.aspx?Cval=6235 | title = Oman Football Association Unveils Official Logo And Kit | website = theweek.co.om | date = 9 May 2012 | access-date = 9 May 2012 }}{{dead link|date=April 2026}}</ref> Later in June 2012, Oman's airline [[Oman Air]] became the official carrier of the [[Oman Football Association]].<ref>{{cite web | url = http://www.omanair.com/about-us/media-center/press-releases-2012/oman-national-team-announce-major-local-sponsor | title = Oman National Team Announce Major Local Sponsor | publisher = Oman Air | date = 7 June 2012 | access-date = 7 June 2012 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20131014053206/http://www.omanair.com/about-us/media-center/press-releases-2012/oman-national-team-announce-major-local-sponsor | archive-date = 14 October 2013 }}</ref>

On 8 February 2014, the Omani Football Association confirmed the tie-up with [[Italy|Italian]] sports apparel manufacturer [[Kappa (company)|Kappa]]. A joint venture agreement was signed by sportswear Kappa and OFA's apparel brand Taj Oman. In a 4-year deal, Kappa will produce the kit worn by all the Oman National football teams bearing the Taj mark and will provide Oman with a range of sportswear specific for the country. The deal will see both the names (Kappa & Taj) on the kit worn by the National teams and on all retail items.<ref>{{cite web | url = https://www.albawaba.com/sport/oman-football-association-confirms-tie-kappa-553389 | title = Oman Football Association confirms tie-up with Kappa | publisher = Al Bawaba | date = 9 February 2014 | access-date = 9 February 2014 | archive-date = 22 December 2017 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20171222085911/https://www.albawaba.com/sport/oman-football-association-confirms-tie-kappa-553389 | url-status = live }}</ref> Oman Air also renewed its deal on the same day with the OFA till the end of the 2013–14 season. On 16 September 2014, the Omani Football Association announced that they had signed an agreement with Asia Sports Marketing to become the exclusive sales agent for the Association.<ref>{{cite web | url = https://www.zawya.com/story/Oman_Football_Association_announce_new_exclusive_sales_partner-ZAWYA20140916104908/ | title = Oman Football Association announce new exclusive sales partner | publisher = Zawya | date = 16 September 2014 | access-date = 16 September 2014 | archive-date = 4 March 2016 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20160304055451/https://www.zawya.com/story/Oman_Football_Association_announce_new_exclusive_sales_partner-ZAWYA20140916104908/ | url-status = live }}</ref> 

On 9 September 2015, the Omani Football Association signed a 1-year contract extension with Oman Air as the official carrier of the team. The association said that although Oman Air's ticket allocation in the deal is primarily meant for the senior national team's tours, OFA has availed the privilege for club teams' trips to Salalah for Omantel Professional League (OPL) matches and for overseas travel of the national age-group squads.<ref>{{cite web | url = http://www.muscatdaily.com/Archive/Sports/Our-partnership-with-Oman-Air-has-grown-says-Sayyid-Khalid-4ahg | title = Our partnership with Oman Air has grown, says Sayyid Khalid | publisher = Muscat Daily | date = 10 September 2015 | access-date = 10 September 2015 | archive-date = 22 December 2017 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20171222053742/http://www.muscatdaily.com/Archive/Sports/Our-partnership-with-Oman-Air-has-grown-says-Sayyid-Khalid-4ahg | url-status = dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url = https://alroya.om/post/143346 | title = الطيران العماني يمدد عقد الشراكة مع اتحاد القدم | publisher = Al Roya | date = 10 September 2015 | access-date = 10 September 2015 | archive-date = 22 December 2017 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20171222051635/https://alroya.om/post/143346 | url-status = live }}</ref> On 18 October 2015, the Omani Football Association announced a partnership with a new mental energizer Energy Drinks Partner, ''Effect''.<ref>{{cite web | url = http://www.ofa.om/oman-football-score-effect-0 | title = Oman Football Score with Effect | publisher = ofa.om | access-date = 20 October 2015 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20151022005005/http://www.ofa.om/oman-football-score-effect-0 | archive-date = 22 October 2015 | url-status = dead | df = dmy-all }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url = http://www.timesofoman.com/article/69815/Sports/Oman/Oman-Football-Association-rope-in-Effect-as-new-energy-drink-partner | title = Oman FA rope in Effect as new energy drink partner | work = Times of Oman | date = 18 October 2015 | access-date = 18 October 2015 | archive-date = 21 October 2015 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20151021121014/http://www.timesofoman.com/article/69815/Sports/Oman/Oman-Football-Association-rope-in-Effect-as-new-energy-drink-partner | url-status = live }}</ref>

| format     = 1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)#Fourth round|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 11 October

| team1      = {{fb-rt|UAE}}

| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288263/288279/400020390
| goals1     =
*[[Marcus Meloni|Meloni]] {{goal|76}}
*[[Caio Lucas|Caio]] {{goal|83}}
| goals2     =

| location   = [[Al Rayyan (city)|Al Rayyan]], Qatar
| stadium    = [[Jassim bin Hamad Stadium]]
| attendance = 8,817
| referee    = [[Alireza Faghani]] ([[Football Australia|Australia]])
| result     = L
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
!style="color:white;background:#DB171B"|Position
!style="color:white;background:#DB171B"|Name
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Ahmed Al-Gharif
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[Tarik Sektioui]]
|-
|Assistant coach
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[Youssouf Hadji]] <br/> {{flagicon|OMA}} Said Al Raqadi <br/> {{flagicon|OMA}} Omar Al-Mushaifri
|-
|Goalkeeping coach
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Maqbool Al-Balushi
|-
|Fitness coach
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Talib Al-Qasmi
|-
|Match analyst
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Waleed Al-Jabri
|-
|Team manager
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Muatasim Al-Balushi
|-
|Players relations manager
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Ahmed Hadid Al-Mukhaini
|-
|Task manager
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Ahmed Al-Owaisi
|-
|Operations manager
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Kamil Al-Balushi
|-
|Photographer
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Salem Al-Muqbali
|-
|Team doctor
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Dr. Mohammed Moulou
|-
|rowspan=2|Physiotherapist
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Said Al-Balushi
|-
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Yaqoob Al-Mahrouqi
|-
|rowspan=2|Masseur
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Farooq Al-Alawi
|-
|{{flagicon|OMA}} Ali Al-Haddad
|}

===Coaching history===
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.
{{div col|colwidth=25em}}
*{{Flagicon|Egypt|1972}} Mohammed Al-Khafaji (1974–1976)
*{{Flagicon|England}} George Smith (1979)
*{{Flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} Hamed El-Dhiab (1980–1982)
*{{Flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} ''Mansaf El-Meliti'' (1982)
*{{Flagicon|Brazil|1968}} [[Paulo Henrique Souza de Oliveira|Paulo de Oliveira]] (1984)
*{{Flagicon|Brazil|1968}} Antônio Clemente (1986)
*{{Flagicon|Brazil|1968}} [[Jorge Vitório]] (1986–1988)
*{{Flagicon|West Germany}} [[Karl-Heinz Heddergott]] (1988–1989)
*{{Flagicon|Germany}} [[Bernd Patzke]] (1990–1992)
*{{Flagicon|Iran}} [[Heshmat Mohajerani]] (1992–1994)
*{{Flagicon|Oman}} [[Rashid Jaber]] (1995–1996)
*{{Flagicon|Egypt}} [[Mahmoud El-Gohary]] (1996)
*{{Flagicon|Slovakia}} [[Jozef Vengloš]] (1996–1997)
*{{Flagicon|Scotland}} [[Ian Porterfield]] (1997)
*{{Flagicon|Iran}} [[Homayoun Shahrokhi]] (1997–1998)
*{{Flagicon|Brazil}} [[Valdeir Vieira]] (1998–1999)
*{{Flagicon|Brazil}} [[Carlos Alberto Torres]] (2000–2001)
*{{Flagicon|Czech Republic}} [[Milan Máčala]] (2001, 2003–2005, 2006–2007)
*{{Flagicon|Germany}} [[Bernd Stange]] (2001)
*{{Flagicon|Oman}} ''[[Rashid Jaber]]'' (2002)
*{{Flagicon|Croatia}} [[Srečko Juričić]] (2005–2006)
*{{Flagicon|Oman}} ''[[Hamad Al-Azani]]'' (2006)
*{{Flagicon|Argentina|1861}} [[Gabriel Calderón]] (2007–2008)
*{{Flagicon|Uruguay}} [[Julio César Ribas]] (2008)
*{{Flagicon|Oman}} ''[[Hamad Al-Azani]]'' (2008)
*{{Flagicon|France|1974}} [[Claude Le Roy]] (2008–2010)
*{{Flagicon|Oman}} [[Hamad Al-Azani]] (2010–2011)
*{{Flagicon|France|1974}} [[Paul Le Guen]] (2011–2015)<ref>{{cite web | url = http://www.timesofoman.com/article/72043/Sports/Oman/Paul-Le-Guen-Oman-national-football-team-coach-sacked:Oman-Football-Association | title = Oman Football Association 'relieves' Le Guen of coaching duties after Oman's loss to Turkmenistan | work = Times of Oman | access-date = 20 November 2015 | archive-date = 23 November 2015 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20151123063444/http://www.timesofoman.com/article/72043/Sports/Oman/Paul-Le-Guen-Oman-national-football-team-coach-sacked:Oman-Football-Association | url-status = live }}</ref>
*{{Flagicon|Spain}} [[Juan Ramón López Caro]] (2016)
*{{Flagicon|Netherlands}} [[Pim Verbeek]] (2016–2019)
*{{Flagicon|Netherlands}} [[Erwin Koeman]] (2019)
*{{Flagicon|Serbia}} [[Goran Stevanović]]  (2020)
*{{Flagicon|Croatia}} [[Branko Ivanković]] (2020–2024)
*{{flagicon|Czechia}} [[Jaroslav Šilhavý]] (2024)
*{{Flagicon|Oman}} [[Rashid Jaber]] (2024–2025)
*{{Flagicon|Portugal}} [[Carlos Queiroz]] (2025–2026)
*{{Flagicon|Morocco}} [[Tarik Sektioui]] (2026–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following 24 players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC fourth round]] matches against [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]] and [[United Arab Emirates national football team|United Arab Emirates]] in October 2025.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://x.com/windowintlpod/status/1973763364137386046/photo/1| title=Here's the Oman squad for the fourth round of Asian FIFA World Cup Qualifiers!|publisher=Oman NT|date=2 October 2025}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 11 October 2025, after the match against [[United Arab Emirates national football team|United Arab Emirates]]''.

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=26|pos=GK|name=[[Ibrahim Al-Mukhaini (footballer, born 1997)|Ibrahim Al-Mukhaini]]|age={{Bda|1996|9|26|df=y}}|caps=49|goals=0|club=[[Al-Nahda Club (Oman)|Al-Nahda]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=GK|name=[[Faiz Al-Rushaidi]]|age={{Bda|1994|9|14|df=y}}|caps=79|goals=0|club=[[Al-Nahda Club (Oman)|Al-Nahda]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=GK|name=[[Bilal Al-Balushi]]|age={{bda|1996|5|29|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Al-Rustaq Club|Al-Rustaq]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=[[Ghanim Al-Habashi]]|age={{bda|1998|8|4|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Al-Nahda Club (Oman)|Al-Nahda]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Thani Al-Rushaidi]]|age={{bda|1995|9|16|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[Al-Nahda Club (Oman)|Al-Nahda]]|clubnat=OMA}}

{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=DF|name=[[Amjad Al-Harthi]]|age={{bda|1994|8|1|df=y}}|caps=40|goals=1|club=[[Al-Nahda Club (Oman)|Al-Nahda]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=DF|name=[[Khalid Al-Braiki]]|age={{Bda|df=yes|1993|7|3}}|caps=54|goals=1|club=[[SK Artis Brno|Artis Brno]]|clubnat=CZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=DF|name=[[Ali Al-Busaidi]]|age={{bda|1991|7|21|df=y}}|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]|caps=106|goals=4|club=[[Al-Seeb Club|Al-Seeb]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=DF|name=[[Mahmood Al-Mushaifri]]|age={{bda|1993|5|14|df=y}}|caps=37|goals=0|club=[[Al-Shabab SC (Seeb)|Al-Shabab]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nayef Faraj]]|age={{Bda|2002|11|4|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Dhofar Club|Dhofar]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=MF|name=[[Arshad Al-Alawi]]|age={{bda|2000|4|12|df=y}}|caps=62|goals=8|club=[[Al-Seeb Club|Al-Seeb]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Zahir Al-Aghbari]]|age={{bda|1999|5|28|df=y}}|caps=58|goals=0|club=[[Al-Seeb Club|Al-Seeb]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Sultan Al-Marzouq]]|age={{Bda|2004|10|23|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Dhofar Club|Dhofar]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=MF|name=[[Abdullah Fawaz]]|age={{bda|1996|7|3|df=y}}|caps=63|goals=7|club=[[Al-Seeb Club|Al-Seeb]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Ahed Al-Mashaiki]]|age={{bda|2003|5|30|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Al-Nahda Club (Oman)|Al-Nahda]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=MF|name=[[Nasser Al-Rawahi]]|age={{Birth date and age|2001|6|26|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=1|club=[[Al-Seeb Club|Al-Seeb]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Salaah Al-Yahyaei]]|age={{Bda|1998|8|17|df=y}}|caps=72|goals=9|club=[[Al-Seeb Club|Al-Seeb]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=MF|name=[[Harib Al-Saadi]]|age={{Bda|1993|9|23|df=y}}|caps=114|goals=1|club=[[Al-Nahda Club (Oman)|Al-Nahda]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=FW|name=[[Issam Al-Sabhi]]|age={{bda|1997|5|1|df=y}}|caps=65|goals=18|club=[[Al-Quwa Al-Jawiya]]|clubnat=IRQ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Mohammed Al-Ghafri]]|age={{bda|1997|5|17|df=y}}|caps=27|goals=4|club=[[Al-Nahda Club (Oman)|Al-Nahda]]|clubnat=OMA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Muhsen Al-Ghassani]]|age={{bda|1997|3|27|df=y}}|caps=73|goals=17|club=[[Bangkok United F.C.|Bangkok United]]|clubnat=THA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=FW|name=[[Abdulrahman Al-Mushaifri]]|age={{bda|1998|6|14|df=y}}|caps=35|goals=8|club=[[SK Artis Brno|Artis Brno]]|clubnat=CZE}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the squad within the last 12 months.

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Ahmed Al-Rawahi]]|age={{Bda|1994|5|5|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Al-Seeb Club|Al-Seeb]]|clubnat=OMA|latest=[[2025 CAFA Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Abdulmalik Al-Badri]]|age={{bda|1998|8|24|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Al-Shabab SC (Seeb)|Al-Shabab]]|clubnat=OMA|latest=v. {{fb|PLE}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Muatasim Al-Wahaibi]]|age={{bda|1998|5|26|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Al-Seeb Club|Al-Seeb]]|clubnat=OMA|latest=v. {{fb|LBN}}, 28 May 2025}} <sup>PRE</sup>
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Ibrahim Al-Rajhi]]|age={{Bda|2000|10|5|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Al-Nasr SC (Salalah)|Al-Nasr]]|clubnat=OMA|latest=[[26th Arabian Gulf Cup]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ahmed Al-Kaabi]]|age={{bda|1996|9|15|df=y}}|caps=52|goals=0|club=[[Al-Nahda Club (Oman)|Al-Nahda]]|clubnat=OMA|latest=[[2025 CAFA Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Juma Al-Habsi]]|age={{bda|1996|1|28|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=0|club=[[Al-Nasr SC (Salalah)|Al-Nasr]]|clubnat=OMA|latest=[[2025 CAFA Nations Cup]]}}

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Omar Al-Malki]]|age={{bda|1994|1|4|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=6|club=[[Al-Seeb Club|Al-Seeb]]|clubnat=OMA|latest=v. {{fb|IRQ}}, 19 November 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Withdrew due to injury
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad
*<sup>WD</sup> = Withdrew due to non-injury related reasons 
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{Updated|8 December 2025}}<ref name="RSSSF">{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Oman - Record International Players |website=[[RSSSF]] |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/oman-recintlp.html |access-date=2 February 2023 |archive-date=9 December 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221209222103/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/oman-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Oman.''

===Asian Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Asian Games|Asian Games]] record
|-
!Year||Round||Pld||W||D||L||GF||GA

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[Arabian Gulf Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (2)''': [[19th Arabian Gulf Cup|2009]], [[23rd Arabian Gulf Cup|2017–18]]
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (4): [[17th Arabian Gulf Cup|2004]], [[18th Arabian Gulf Cup|2007]], [[25th Arabian Gulf Cup|2023]], [[26th Arabian Gulf Cup|2024–25]]
* '''[[WAFF Championship]]'''
** {{Bronze3}} Third Place (1): [[2012 WAFF Championship|2012]]

===Awards===
* '''[[Arabian Gulf Cup|Arabian Gulf Cup Fair Play Award]] (1)''': 2014
